lesson.menu_desc: Look back on the year and look ahead -- review your milestones and achievements with reported speech, and share your plans for next year -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 20 -- Year-End Review -- Looking Back & Looking Ahead
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Percival JR | Aula 20 -- Year-End Review

listenings.text: Hi Percival, this is Karen, and congratulations -- you have reached a real milestone: twenty lessons together. Let me look back on the year with you, because your growth has been clear. At the start, you told me that meetings were difficult and that presentations made you nervous. Look at you now. You said the audit reports were hard to write, and today you write them and present them aloud to the board. That is a big achievement, and you should be proud. You also told me that emails took you a long time. You said you were not confident, but you overcame that, and now you reply in minutes. Your strength this year was consistency: you came to every class and you did the work. The biggest improvement was your speaking. At first you said you needed every word on paper; now you speak freely. I am grateful for your effort, Percival. So, before we look ahead, take a moment and look back. You have come a long way on this journey, and the best part is still in front of you.

When I think about this year, I feel proud. It has been a real milestone for me. At the start, I told Karen that English was my weakest skill, and that meetings and presentations were a struggle. I said I was not confident. But I have changed. My biggest achievement is that I now present the audit report to the board in English, and I am calm. My biggest improvement is my speaking, because I no longer need every word on paper. My strength this year was simple: I did the work every week, and I overcame my fear of speaking. I am grateful to Karen for her patience and her clear feedback. Now I want to look ahead. Next year, I am going to lead an international audit project, and I will present the results to our partners abroad. I am going to read one business book in English every month, and I will keep this same routine, because growth never stops. Looking back, I have learned a lot. Looking ahead, I am ready for more.

extra_audio.text: First, Percival looks back on the year and sees how much his English has grown. Then he reviews his biggest achievement: he now presents the audit report to the board in English. After that, he reports what Karen told him at the start, using reported speech: she said that meetings were difficult and that presentations made him nervous. Later, he names his strength and his biggest improvement, and he overcomes his old fear of speaking. Finally, he looks ahead and shares his plans for next year: he is going to lead an international project and he will keep the same routine, because growth never stops.
